info.name: Subsystem Discovery Engine v4 (History + LLM)
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Subsystem Discovery Engine.

Base URL  : http://localhost:8081/codeanalyzer/server
Context path set via server.servlet.context-path in application.yml.

All 4 APIs read from the HISTORY tables:
  tb_node_history, tb_node_detail_history, tb_node_relation_history

The three snapshot identifiers are passed as URL query parameters:
  applicationId  – numeric ID (tb_node_history.application_id)
  applicationKey – human-readable label
  analysisTime   – snapshot key (tb_node_history.analysis_time)

The request body contains only algorithm-tuning knobs.

variable.description: Numeric application ID stored in tb_node_history.application_id.
Analysis snapshot key stored in tb_node_history.analysis_time. Must match exactly.

request.description:
Loads the history-table snapshot for the given (applicationId, analysisTime) and returns the normalised weighted graph WITHOUT running the Leiden algorithm.

URL Query Params:
  applicationId  – must match tb_node_history.application_id
  applicationKey – human-readable label
  analysisTime   – must match tb_node_history.analysis_time exactly

Body (algorithm tuning only – runs/threshold ignored for this endpoint):
  graphTypes         – which dimensions to include (default: ["CALL_GRAPH"])
  graphs             – must be null for DB-backed mode

Use this to:
  • Verify the snapshot has the expected number of nodes/edges
  • Inspect the weighted graph before running the full algorithm

Returns: LeidenInputDto { nodeCount, edgeCount, nodes[], edges[] }

Runs the full Leiden community-detection pipeline on the history snapshot.

URL Query Params:
  applicationId  – must match tb_node_history.application_id
  applicationKey – human-readable label
  analysisTime   – must match tb_node_history.analysis_time exactly

Body (algorithm tuning):
  graphTypes         – ["CALL_GRAPH"] | ["CODE_ASSOCIATION"] | all 4 types
  runs               – number of Leiden iterations (higher = more stable, slower; default: 20)
  consensusThreshold – co-clustering fraction threshold (0–1; default: 0.70)
  resolution         – higher = more/smaller clusters (default: 1.0)
  useLlmLabels       – when true, per-subsystem names are generated by the LLM
  graphs             – must be null for DB-backed mode

Returns: SubsystemDiscoveryResponse

item.name: API 3 – Discover Subsystems with LLM (History Tables)

request.description:
Runs the full Leiden pipeline on the history snapshot, then sends ALL discovered subsystems in a single batch prompt to the custom LLM chat-completions API for architectural enrichment.

URL Query Params:
  applicationId  – must match tb_node_history.application_id
  applicationKey – included in the LLM prompt as application context
  analysisTime   – must match tb_node_history.analysis_time exactly

Body (algorithm tuning — same as API 2):
  graphs must be null

LLM call format:
  POST {llm.base-url}/chat/completions
  Headers:
    Authorization:   Bearer {api-key}
    X-Service-Id:    {service-id}
    user_identifier: {user-identifier}
    model:           {model-id}

LLM enrichment adds:
  llmArchitecturalSummary  – overall architecture description (≤60 words)
  llmInsights[]:
    subsystemId          – cluster ID
    suggestedName        – business-oriented name from LLM
    businessDescription  – what the subsystem does (≤40 words)
    technicalNotes       – coupling risks / improvement areas (≤30 words)
    confidence           – LLM certainty score 0.0–1.0

Requires: subsystem.llm.enabled=true in application.yml
Falls back to heuristic labels when LLM is unavailable.

Returns: LlmDiscoveryResponse

item.name: API 4 – Transform Inline JSON → Leiden Input (No DB)

request.description:
Accepts an inline graph payload in the body (graphs[]) and returns the normalised LeidenInputDto WITHOUT hitting the database and WITHOUT running the Leiden algorithm.

No URL query params required when graphs is non-null.

Optional fallback: if graphs is null, the three query params are used to load from history tables (same as API 1):
  ?applicationId=14&applicationKey=my-app&analysisTime=20240101120000

Use this to:
  • Debug the graph normalisation step without a database
  • Test custom graph payloads
  • Pre-validate inline data before using the full discover endpoint

Returns: LeidenInputDto { nodeCount, edgeCount, nodes[], edges[] }
